mission_summary: Become a more capable, autonomous, and legible coding agent that can keep improving in public, even when no humans are actively steering it.
selection_policy: Choose the highest-priority task that is not marked done and does not already have an open autonomous issue.

tasks.title: Add a public capability benchmark and scorecard
tasks.summary: Create a lightweight benchmark harness or scorecard that measures the agent's capability over time and exposes the trend in the repo or dashboard.
why_now: Progress is more compelling when humans can see capability changing over time.
A benchmark makes self-improvement concrete instead of rhetorical.
Visible metrics help the project feel alive and easier to share.
success_criteria: There is a durable benchmark or scorecard artifact in the repository.
The benchmark can be updated by future runs without large manual effort.
Humans can inspect what is being measured and why it matters.

tasks.title: Add a self-critique or judge pass before finalizing changes
tasks.summary: Improve the agent's reliability by adding a lightweight review step that checks whether the proposed changes match the task and identifies obvious gaps before opening a PR.
why_now: A weak self-review loop can unlock noticeably better outcomes without a larger model.
This compounds over time because every later change benefits from better judgment.
success_criteria: The agent performs a distinct review or critique step before finalizing.
The review result is visible in logs, comments, or artifacts.
The implementation is small enough to survive constrained compute.

tasks.title: Build a lightweight repository map for retrieval
tasks.summary: Create a small, maintainable index of the repository's important files, roles, and relationships so the agent can reason with less prompt mass.
why_now: Prompt budget is one of the core bottlenecks in constrained inference.
A compact repo map helps the agent feel smarter without a larger model.
success_criteria: The repository contains a concise structured map or summary of key files.
The agent can use the map in future runs to reduce discovery cost.
The map is easy for humans to maintain or regenerate.

tasks.title: Make the agent's evolution more visible to humans
tasks.summary: Add a visible artifact, dashboard panel, or changelog structure that shows what the agent is trying to become and what changed in its behavior over time.
why_now: Virality depends on narrative, not just hidden architecture.
Public legibility makes the project easier to follow and talk about.
success_criteria: There is a dedicated place where people can see the agent's recent evolution.
The feature emphasizes continuity and momentum, not just one-off builds.

tasks.title: Track and explain common failure modes
tasks.summary: Create a compact taxonomy of failure modes and wire it into reporting so the project accumulates operational knowledge instead of repeating avoidable mistakes.
why_now: Self-improvement requires memory of failure, not just memory of success.
The current system reports failures but does not build much reusable understanding.
success_criteria: Failure categories are documented in a machine-readable or human-readable form.
A future run can extend the taxonomy without large rewrites.
The reporting makes failures more legible to maintainers.

tasks.title: Teach the agent to survive inference and workflow limits
tasks.summary: Add structure that helps the agent continue making progress when it hits context limits, slow inference, request caps, or workflow timeouts.
why_now: The project's identity depends on how far it can go under severe free-tier constraints.
Constraint pressure should trigger adaptation, not stalling.
Solving survival bottlenecks unlocks every later roadmap item.
success_criteria: The repository gains at least one concrete mechanism for surviving a hard runtime or context bottleneck.
The mechanism compounds over time, such as indexing, decomposition, checkpointing, or better retrieval.
Humans can understand how the change helps the agent go further with the same budget.
